goal: Add a readable and playable entrance into the actual nucleus, distinct from the removed outer cell entry helpers.
changes: Added a NucleusEntryPortal to the live Workspace.Nucleus_Model when the nucleus is large enough for player traversal.
Placed the portal on the side of the nucleus facing the submarine/player approach.
Added an organic rim, glow, light, and short bubble/beam trail using non-colliding parts.
Changed the nucleus collision mode from a simple closed ImportedProxy to PortalControlledProxy for the main nucleus.
Added a runtime collision controller that keeps the nucleus proxy solid by default, then opens it when the player is near the portal or already inside the nucleus.
Kept the recently removed outer cell entrance and generated Terrain systems removed.

studio_probe.rebuild_stability: Portal center stayed unchanged across two consecutive require(builder).build() calls.

validation: Deployed BuildMainCellExperience.lua into Roblox Studio through the legacy bridge.
Ran require(builder).build() twice and confirmed the portal did not drift between rebuilds.
note: This is a Roblox-side gameplay portal over the current imported nucleus mesh. A future Blender source pass should cut a matching organic doorway into the exported visual/collision mesh for highest fidelity.
result: The player now has a distinct nucleus entrance marker and a portal-controlled collision path into the actual nucleus.
